basic_details.job_title: TGPSC DIET Lecturer Notification 2026 OUT - Apply Online for 86 Lecturers and Senior Lecturer Posts
basic_details.post_name: Senior Lecturer in DIET & Lecturer in DIET
basic_details.short_information: The Telangana Public Service Commission (TGPSC), formerly known as TSPSC, has rolled out a fresh recruitment drive for teaching positions in District Institutes of Education and Training (DIET) under the School Education Department. This TSPSC DIET Lecturer notification - officially Notification No. 04/G/SE/2026, dated 18/06/2026 - opens up 86 posts spread across Senior Lecturer and Lecturer cadres in 19 different subject categories. Online applications open on 24th June 2026 and close on 29th July 2026 at 5:00 PM, with the written examination tentatively scheduled for November 2026.

important_dates.admit_card_date: 7 days before the exam, till 5:00 PM on the previous day

application_fee.details: The fee structure has two separate components - an application processing fee and an examination fee - and both must be paid online through the payment gateway. | Note: Government/PSU/Corporation employees of any kind must pay the examination fee regardless of category. Once paid, the fee is non-refundable and non-adjustable under any circumstances.

age_limit.details: Age is calculated as on 01/07/2026. This is one of the more candidate-friendly aspects of this notification, since the upper age limit has been substantially revised upward. | Minimum Age: 18 years (must not be born after 01/07/2008) Maximum Age: 44 years (must not be born before 02/07/1982) | Important update: As per G.O. Ms. No. 86, General Administration (Ser.A) Department, dated 18/05/2026, the upper age limit for this post was raised from 34 years to 44 years - a 10-year increase - significantly widening the eligible candidate pool. | No candidate is eligible if they have crossed the superannuation age of 61 years, even after availing relaxation.

qualification.details: Educational qualification for this recruitment must be completed and the result formally declared by the university on or before the notification date, i.e., 18/06/2026 - mere appearance in the final exam or pending results will not be accepted. | Across almost all 19 post codes, the DIET Lecturer qualification in Telangana follows a similar two-part structure: | For posts requiring Urdu or Telugu medium eligibility, candidates must have either passed SSC/Intermediate/Degree in that medium or studied the language as First Language/Part-I/Second Language/subject at the respective level. | Allied subjects (such as Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Biochemistry, Biotechnology, Geoinformatics, etc.) are accepted as per Annexure-I of G.O. Ms. No. 11, School Education (Ser.III) Department, dated 04/03/2023. | DSSSB Recruitment 2026 – 1979 Vacancies for TGT, JSA, IT Assistant & Other Posts

vacancy_details.Name of the Post: Senior Lecturer, DIET (Pre-Service/In-Service Programme, Field Interaction, DRU, Planning & Management, Educational Technology, Work Experience, Curriculum Material Development & Evaluation)
Lecturer – In-Service Training, Field Interaction & Coordination, Work Experience, Curriculum Material Development & Evaluation, DRU

post_wise_breakdown.name_of_the_post: Senior Lecturer, DIET (Pre-Service/In-Service Programme, Field Interaction, DRU, Planning & Management, Educational Technology, Work Experience, Curriculum Material Development & Evaluation)
Lecturer – In-Service Training, Field Interaction & Coordination, Work Experience, Curriculum Material Development & Evaluation, DRU

salary_details.details: A common question among aspirants tracking this Telangana DIET Lecturer notification is what these posts actually pay once selected. The notification specifies two separate pay bands depending on the cadre. | Senior Lecturer, DIET (Post Code 1): Rs. 58,850 – Rs. 1,37,050 per month Lecturer, DIET (Post Codes 2 to 19): Rs. 54,220 – Rs. 1,33,630 per month

selection_process: Selection is made purely on the marks secured in the written examination
followed by certificate verification and a medical fitness check. Here's how the qualifying criteria work:
In the event SC/ST candidates do not meet the prescribed qualifying marks
they may still be considered based on their relative performance

irrespective of the minimum marks. Securing the qualifying marks does not automatically guarantee selection - final allotment depends on merit rank and post preference (web-options) against available vacancies.

exam_pattern.details: The selection is based purely on a written objective-type examination, conducted either in OMR mode or as a Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT), at the Commission's discretion. | Both Paper-I and Paper-II (except for language subjects) are conducted bilingually in English and Telugu.

syllabus.details: The syllabus for Paper-I is common across all post codes, while Paper-II varies subject-wise. Here's a quick summary of what each paper covers: | Paper-I – General Studies & General Abilities covers current affairs, Indian and Telangana history/geography/economy, the Indian Constitution, social exclusion and inclusive policies, Telangana culture and policies, logical reasoning, and basic English. | Paper-II, Part A – Education: Theory and Practice (common for Post Codes 1–18) covers philosophical and sociological foundations of education, child psychology and development, pedagogy, curriculum, assessment & evaluation, ICT in education, and basic educational research methods. | Paper-II, Part B is subject-specific - for example, English Lecturer candidates are tested on English literature, grammar, and language pedagogy, while Maths Lecturer candidates are tested on algebra, calculus, statistics, and maths pedagogy, and so on for each of the 19 post codes.

how_to_apply: Applying for this recruitment involves registering through One Time Registration (OTR) first, followed by the actual application submission. Here is the process broken into simple steps:
Visit the official TGPSC website (tgpsc.gov.in) and complete One Time Registration (OTR) if you haven't registered before. This generates your TGPSC ID.
Log in using your TGPSC ID and Date of Birth, verify the OTP sent to your registered mobile number.
Verify auto-fetched details such as Name, DOB, Educational Qualification, Community, Gender, and Disability status from your OTR profile.
Preview the application carefully, then click "Save & Payment" to proceed.
Pay the prescribed fee via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI.
Download the generated PDF application for future reference - note the Reference ID Number printed on it.
Apply Online through the official TGPSC portal once the application window opens on 24/06/2026.
Remember: OTR registration itself is not considered an application. You must separately submit the online application specifically for this notification before the deadline.

content_sections.heading: TGPSC (TSPSC) DIET Lecturer Recruitment 2026: Quick Overview

rows.details: Telangana Public Service Commission (TGPSC, formerly TSPSC)
Senior Lecturer in DIET & Lecturer in DIET
OMR-based Offline or Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT)

content_sections.heading: TGPSC DIET Lecturer Notification 2026: PDF Download Link
content_sections.text: Candidates are strongly advised to read the complete official notification before applying, since it contains the full text of every rule, Government Order, and annexure referenced in this article. PDF Download Link : Available on the official TGPSC website (tgpsc.gov.in) under the "Notifications" section - Notification No. 04/G/SE/2026, dated 18/06/2026.

content_sections.heading: Important Dates for TGPSC DIET Lecturer Notification 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates planning to apply for this Telangana DIET Lecturer notification should keep these dates noted, since TGPSC does not entertain late submissions or requests for extension under any circumstances.

rows.event: Last Date to Submit Online Application

rows.date_timeline: 7 days before the exam, till 5:00 PM on the previous day

content_sections.heading: TGPSC DIET Lecturer Vacancy Details 2026 (Post Code Wise)
content_sections.text: The 86 vacancies in this DIET Lecturer vacancy 2026 drive are distributed across 19 post codes, covering subjects ranging from Education and English to Maths, Science, Social Studies, Urdu, Art Education, and Physical Education. Each post is further categorised under Multi-Zone-I and Multi-Zone-II based on Telangana's local cadre system. Note: As clarified in the notification, unfilled or non-joining vacancies will be carried forward to the next recruitment cycle, and any addition or deletion of vacancies will be communicated through an official addendum before the exam or before the final selection list, respectively.

rows.name_of_the_post: Senior Lecturer, DIET (Pre-Service/In-Service Programme, Field Interaction, DRU, Planning & Management, Educational Technology, Work Experience, Curriculum Material Development & Evaluation)
Lecturer – In-Service Training, Field Interaction & Coordination, Work Experience, Curriculum Material Development & Evaluation, DRU

content_sections.heading: TGPSC DIET Lecturer Salary & Pay Scale
content_sections.text: A common question among aspirants tracking this Telangana DIET Lecturer notification is what these posts actually pay once selected. The notification specifies two separate pay bands depending on the cadre. Senior Lecturer, DIET (Post Code 1): Rs. 58,850 – Rs. 1,37,050 per month Lecturer, DIET (Post Codes 2 to 19): Rs. 54,220 – Rs. 1,33,630 per month

content_sections.heading: TGPSC DIET Lecturer Eligibility & Qualification in Telangana 2026
content_sections.text: Educational qualification for this recruitment must be completed and the result formally declared by the university on or before the notification date, i.e., 18/06/2026 - mere appearance in the final exam or pending results will not be accepted. Across almost all 19 post codes, the DIET Lecturer qualification in Telangana follows a similar two-part structure: For posts requiring Urdu or Telugu medium eligibility, candidates must have either passed SSC/Intermediate/Degree in that medium or studied the language as First Language/Part-I/Second Language/subject at the respective level. Allied subjects (such as Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Biochemistry, Biotechnology, Geoinformatics, etc.) are accepted as per Annexure-I of G.O. Ms. No. 11, School Education (Ser.III) Department, dated 04/03/2023. DSSSB Recruitment 2026 – 1979 Vacancies for TGT, JSA, IT Assistant & Other Posts

rows.pg_subject_required: Philosophy, Psychology, Sociology, Telugu, English, Urdu, Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Chemistry, Botany, Zoology, History, Economics, Political Science, Public Administration, Geography, Adult Education, Electronics (or allied subjects) + M.Ed.
Same as above + Urdu medium eligibility (studied/passed in Urdu medium)
PG in English + M.Ed. with English methodology
PG in Telugu + M.Ed. with Telugu methodology + Telugu medium eligibility
PG in Urdu + M.Ed. with Urdu methodology + Urdu medium eligibility
PG in Mathematics (or allied) + M.Ed. with Maths methodology
PG in Physics/Chemistry/Botany/Zoology (or allied) + M.Ed. with Physical/Biological Science methodology
PG in History/Economics/Political Science/Public Administration/Geography (or allied) + M.Ed. with Social Studies methodology
PG in Fine Arts (Applied Art) OR any PG + separate Degree in Fine Arts

content_sections.heading: Age Limit for TGPSC DIET Lecturer Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Age is calculated as on 01/07/2026 . This is one of the more candidate-friendly aspects of this notification, since the upper age limit has been substantially revised upward. Minimum Age: 18 years (must not be born after 01/07/2008) Maximum Age: 44 years (must not be born before 02/07/1982) Important update: As per G.O. Ms. No. 86, General Administration (Ser.A) Department, dated 18/05/2026, the upper age limit for this post was raised from 34 years to 44 years - a 10-year increase - significantly widening the eligible candidate pool. No candidate is eligible if they have crossed the superannuation age of 61 years, even after availing relaxation.

rows.relaxation_permitted: Up to 5 years (based on length of regular service)
3 years + length of service in armed forces
3 years + length of service in NCC

content_sections.heading: Application Fee for TGPSC DIET Lecturer Notification 2026
content_sections.text: The fee structure has two separate components - an application processing fee and an examination fee - and both must be paid online through the payment gateway. Note: Government/PSU/Corporation employees of any kind must pay the examination fee regardless of category. Once paid, the fee is non-refundable and non-adjustable under any circumstances.

content_sections.heading: TGPSC DIET Lecturer Exam Pattern 2026
content_sections.text: The selection is based purely on a written objective-type examination, conducted either in OMR mode or as a Computer Based Recruitment Test (CBRT), at the Commission's discretion. Both Paper-I and Paper-II (except for language subjects) are conducted bilingually in English and Telugu .

content_sections.heading: Exam Centres for TGPSC DIET Lecturer Recruitment
content_sections.text: The objective-type examination will be conducted at the following centres, subject to change at the Commission's discretion: Hyderabad (including HMDA Jurisdiction) Karimnagar Khammam Warangal Nizamabad Requests to change the allotted exam centre will not be entertained.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process & Qualifying Marks
content_sections.text: Selection is made purely on the marks secured in the written examination, followed by certificate verification and a medical fitness check. Here's how the qualifying criteria work: In the event SC/ST candidates do not meet the prescribed qualifying marks, they may still be considered based on their relative performance, irrespective of the minimum marks. Securing the qualifying marks does not automatically guarantee selection - final allotment depends on merit rank and post preference (web-options) against available vacancies.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for TGPSC DIET Lecturer Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Applying for this recruitment involves registering through One Time Registration (OTR) first, followed by the actual application submission. Here is the process broken into simple steps: Visit the official TGPSC website (tgpsc.gov.in) and complete One Time Registration (OTR) if you haven't registered before. This generates your TGPSC ID. Log in using your TGPSC ID and Date of Birth, verify the OTP sent to your registered mobile number. Verify auto-fetched details such as Name, DOB, Educational Qualification, Community, Gender, and Disability status from your OTR profile. Select your preferred examination centre. Preview the application carefully, then click "Save & Payment" to proceed. Pay the prescribed fee via Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. Download the generated PDF application for future reference - note the Reference ID Number printed on it. Apply Online through the official TGPSC portal once the application window opens on 24/06/2026. Remember: OTR registration itself is not considered an application. You must separately submit the online application specifically for this notification before the deadline.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required During TGPSC DIET Lecturer Recruitment
content_sections.text: Candidates should keep the following documents ready for upload or verification at various stages of the recruitment process: Recent colour passport-size photograph (within 3 months) and scanned signature Proof of educational qualification (PG degree, M.Ed. degree) SSC/CBSE/ICSE certificate (for Date of Birth proof) Study certificate from 1st to 7th Class (for local status/residence proof) Community certificate (for SC/ST/BC candidates) issued by a Tahsildar or above Non-Creamy Layer Certificate (for BC candidates, Form-VIIB) Income certificate (for EWS candidates) Disability certificate in SADAREM format (for PwD candidates) No Objection Certificate (for already-employed candidates) Declaration of Unemployment (for fee exemption) Aadhaar Card / Voter ID / PAN Card or any other valid photo ID HPPSC Assistant Professor Recruitment 2026 Notification Out for 369 Posts

content_sections.heading: Reservation Policy in TGPSC DIET Lecturer Notification
content_sections.text: Reservation in this recruitment follows Rule 22 and 22(A) of the Telangana State and Subordinate Service Rules, 1996, along with multiple Government Orders covering SC, ST, BC, EWS, Persons with Disabilities, and Women candidates. A few key points to note: Local Reservation: 95% of posts under both Multi Zone-I and Multi Zone-II are reserved for local candidates as per G.O. Ms. No. 124, GA (SPF-MC) Department, dated 30/08/2018. Candidates availing reservation/age relaxation benefits under SC/ST/BC/EWS/PH categories are also automatically considered for Open Category (OC) vacancies. Sports reservation (2%) applies as per G.O. Ms. No. 74, Youth Advancement, Tourism & Culture (Sports) Department. Candidates from outside Telangana State are not entitled to any reservation benefits.

content_sections.heading: TGPSC DIET Lecturer Syllabus Highlights
content_sections.text: The syllabus for Paper-I is common across all post codes, while Paper-II varies subject-wise. Here's a quick summary of what each paper covers: Paper-I – General Studies & General Abilities covers current affairs, Indian and Telangana history/geography/economy, the Indian Constitution, social exclusion and inclusive policies, Telangana culture and policies, logical reasoning, and basic English. Paper-II, Part A – Education: Theory and Practice (common for Post Codes 1–18) covers philosophical and sociological foundations of education, child psychology and development, pedagogy, curriculum, assessment & evaluation, ICT in education, and basic educational research methods. Paper-II, Part B is subject-specific - for example, English Lecturer candidates are tested on English literature, grammar, and language pedagogy, while Maths Lecturer candidates are tested on algebra, calculus, statistics, and maths pedagogy, and so on for each of the 19 post codes.

faq.question: Q1. What is TGPSC Notification No. 04/G/SE/2026 about?
faq.answer: It is a general recruitment notification issued by the Telangana Public Service Commission for filling 86 posts of Senior Lecturer in DIET and Lecturer in DIET under the School Education Department, Telangana.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in TGPSC DIET Lecturer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 86 total vacancies across 19 post codes, covering subjects like Education, English, Telugu, Urdu, Maths, Science, Social Studies, Psychology, Philosophy, Art Education, Educational Technology, and Physical Education.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for TGPSC DIET Lecturer posts?
faq.answer: The online application window closes on 29th July 2026 at 5:00 PM. Applications submitted after this deadline will not be accepted under any circumstances.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 18 years and the maximum age is 44 years as on 01/07/2026. The upper limit was raised from 34 to 44 years via G.O. Ms. No. 86 dated 18/05/2026.

faq.question: Q5. What educational qualification is required?
faq.answer: Generally, a Postgraduate degree (55% marks, 50% for SC/ST/BC/PwD) in the relevant subject along with an M.Ed. degree (55%/50% marks) from an NCTE-recognised institution. The Physical Education post requires only a PG in Physical Education.

faq.question: Q6. What is the application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: The application processing fee is Rs. 1,000 for OC/BC candidates and Rs. 500 for SC/ST/PwD candidates. An additional examination fee of Rs. 120 applies, except for unemployed candidates who are exempted.

faq.question: Q7. What is the exam pattern for TGPSC DIET Lecturer Recruitment?
faq.answer: The exam consists of Paper-I (General Studies & General Abilities, 150 marks) and Paper-II (Concerned Subject, divided into Part A and Part B, 150 marks each), making a total of 450 marks. It is an objective-type test conducted in OMR or CBRT mode.

faq.question: Q8. Where will the TGPSC DIET Lecturer exam be conducted?
faq.answer: The exam will be held at five centres: Hyderabad (including HMDA jurisdiction), Karimnagar, Khammam, Warangal, and Nizamabad.

faq.question: Q9. What is the salary for Senior Lecturer and Lecturer DIET posts?
faq.answer: Senior Lecturer (Post Code 1) carries a pay scale of Rs. 58,850–1,37,050, while Lecturer posts (Post Codes 2–19) carry a pay scale of Rs. 54,220–1,33,630.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply online for this recruitment?
faq.answer: You need to first complete One Time Registration (OTR) on tgpsc.gov.in, then log in with your TGPSC ID, fill the application form, pay the fee online, and download the submitted PDF application for future reference.
